content.description:
The kernel is Hobson's minimal runtime—the smallest possible core that enables everything else to be built as items.

## Philosophy

The kernel follows a **minimal design** principle: it provides only what cannot be implemented as userland items. Everything else—views, editors, error handling, search—lives outside the kernel as regular items that can be inspected, modified, or replaced.

## What the Kernel Does

| Responsibility | Why It Must Be Kernel |
|----------------|----------------------|
| **Storage** | Items need somewhere to live before items can define storage |
| **Type validation** | Must validate type chains before items can run |
| **Code execution** | Must load code items before code items can load other code |
| **View dispatch** | Must find and run views before views can render anything |
| **Bootstrap** | Must initialize the system before system items exist |

## The 6 Kernel Modules

The kernel is decomposed into cohesive modules, each stored as an item:

```query
const modules = await api.query({ type: api.IDS.KERNEL_MODULE });
modules.sort((a, b) => (a.name || '').localeCompare(b.name || ''));
const rows = modules.map(m => {
  const desc = m.content?.description?.split('\n')[0] || 'No description';
  return `| [${m.name}](item://${m.id}) | ${desc} |`;
});
return '| Module | Purpose |\n|--------|---------|\n' + rows.join('\n') + '\n';
```

## What's NOT in the Kernel

These are implemented as userland items:

- **Error handling** — [default-error-handler](item://e7707000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002) creates error items
- **Selection** — [selection-manager](item://f1111111-0001-0000-0000-000000000000) tracks selected items
- **Item search** — Libraries and views, not kernel code
- **Markdown rendering** — [hobson-markdown](item://a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-e1f2a3b4c5d6) library
- **All views** — Even the viewport view is technically userland
- **All type definitions** — Types like `note`, `container`, `tag` are items

## Self-Modification

Because kernel modules are items, you can:
- Read their source code
- Link to specific functions via `item://id#code?region=name`
- (Carefully) modify kernel behavior
- Replace modules with custom implementations

**Warning:** Editing kernel modules can break your system. Use [safe mode](item://c0c0c0c0-0007-0000-0000-000000000000) to recover.

## Design Rationale

### Why Minimal?

The kernel follows the principle: if it can be built as a userland item, it should be. This means:
- Less kernel code to break
- More of the system is inspectable and modifiable
- Features can be upgraded without touching the kernel
- The system increasingly defines itself in terms of itself

### Why Always-On?

Hobson embodies the \"always on\" principle from Conway's Humane Dozen. The app runs continuously (like Smalltalk) — no start/stop cycle. Modifying a code item changes running behavior immediately.

### Lifecycle Hooks

The kernel manages automatic subscriptions: when a view renders an item, the kernel subscribes to changes in that item's attachments. When a child changes, the kernel calls the parent's lifecycle hooks (if defined) and automatically re-renders. See [Event System](item://c0c0c0c0-0006-0000-0000-000000000000) for details.

## See Also

- [Bootstrap](item://c0c0c0c0-0002-0000-0000-000000000000) — How the system starts
- [REPL](item://c0c0c0c0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) — The interactive console and API
- [Safe Mode](item://c0c0c0c0-0007-0000-0000-000000000000) — Recovery when things break
- [Error Handling](item://c0c0c0c0-0023-0000-0000-000000000000) — Three-tier error system
- [kernel:item](item://00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — The universal item structure
